_meta.notes: Lead flycatcher at Mudmoth. Up before dawn for the eggs; counts them by feel.

tagline: lacquered net resting across a bony shoulder; steady-eyed.

firstMeet.subject: you didn't startle the eggs
firstMeet.body:
Most who come past the lacquer-shed in the dawn hour startle the eggs. The eggs are very particular. You walked past quietly and the count for the morning was the count I expected.

This is not a small thing. Whoever taught you to walk quiet did not waste the teaching.

If you come back, come at dawn. I will not have time to talk, but I will set out a cup.

— Tam

metadata.voice: low, slow, the calm of someone who has not raised their voice in twenty years
